title: Pilot protocol end-to-end: genomic DNA extraction (low hazard), fully reviewed
context: M0's exit gate requires one low-hazard protocol shipped through the full pipeline — schema-valid, linted, license+ethics gate passed, dual-use screen clear, technical and safety sign-off recorded, rendered outputs produced. Genomic DNA extraction is the chosen pilot because it is a common, foundational cancer-research method with low-to-moderate hazard (chaotropic reagents, ethanol) and multiple openly-licensed source protocols available. This task proves the entire pipeline end-to-end before the seed library scales up.
objective: Author and ship a complete, schema-valid genomic DNA extraction protocol through the full lab-protocols-open pipeline — license+ethics gate, dual-use screen, schema validation, provenance linting, technical review, credentialed safety sign-off, semver v1.0.0, changelog, and rendered Markdown/HTML outputs.
acceptanceCriteria: One low-hazard protocol (genomic DNA extraction) authored to schema-001; schema-valid and linter-green.
100% of steps and safety claims carry resolvable, license-verified provenance references.
Full safety section present: PPE, hazards (chaotropic reagents, ethanol, etc.), waste disposal, spill/emergency response — with credentialed safety sign-off from the reviewer secured in reviewer-002.
License+Ethics gate PASS recorded with evidence (license id, URL, snapshot SHA-256, permitsDerivatives); dual-use screen clear.
All mandatory disclaimers present (RUO + institutional biosafety/EHS/IBC/IACUC/IRB notice).
semver v1.0.0 + CHANGELOG; rendered Markdown/HTML outputs produced; commit DCO signed-off; CI green.
resources: NIH/CDC BMBL 6th ed. (biosafety levels and safety guidance)
output: A committed, schema-valid, fully-reviewed genomic DNA extraction protocol record (protocols/genomic-dna-extraction/v1.0.0/protocol.yaml + CHANGELOG.md) with rendered Markdown/HTML outputs, license evidence, and all review sign-offs recorded; CI green; commit DCO signed-off.
